findings.title: Malformed reviewer-output refs can panic writeback validation
findings.rationale: parse_reviewer_output_evidence_ref accepts output IDs that become invalid path components after trimming (for example, `.json` -> empty, or `..`), and load_reviewer_output_from_evidence_ref forwards that value into MissionPaths::reviewer_output. MissionPaths uses checked_id_component(...).expect(...), so invalid components trigger a panic instead of a structured invalid-evidence error.
findings.suggested_next_action: Validate both parsed bundle_id and output_id with validate_id_component before calling MissionPaths::reviewer_output (and reject empty post-trim output IDs), then add a regression test that record-review-outcome returns an error for refs like reviewer-output:<bundle>:.json and reviewer-output:<bundle>:.. without panicking.
